reviewer: hand-authored (Opus) — runnability verification + driven state/matrix run + light/dark screenshots

rubric.evidence: Runnability verified: (1) Transformers.js 3.7.5 registers GraniteForCausalLM / GraniteModel (grep of the dist bundle), so model_type 'granite' loads through the standard text-generation pipeline — no 'Unsupported model type'. (2) A headless-Chrome smoke against pipeline('text-generation','onnx-community/granite-3.0-2b-instruct',{device:'wasm',dtype:'q8'}) downloaded config/tokenizer/onnx and began loading with NO error. (3) The demo runs the EXACT same worker/pipeline code path as the falcon3 sibling, which produced real streamed output in the same harness, and as the qwen2.5-0.5b-wasm reference. (4) onnx-community/granite-3.0-2b-instruct ships onnx/model_quantized.onnx(+_data) (q8) and onnx/model_q4f16.onnx matching the working WASM/WebGPU pattern.
rubric.notes: A full end-to-end generation was NOT captured in the headless sandbox because the q8 build is a ~2.5 GB download (2B model; onnx/model_quantized.onnx_data alone is ~2.5 GB) which exceeded the sandbox bandwidth/time window in this pass — NOT a model failure (the sibling falcon3, 1.7 GB, streamed real output; granite's load began cleanly with no error). On a WebGPU device the worker uses the smaller ~1.6 GB q4f16 build. Capturing the streamed output on a faster connection / GPU device is the one open item (openQuestion) — no output is faked meanwhile; the page shows the honest absent→Download state until a real run completes.

rubric.evidence: Expected shape is correct and legibly surfaced: the chat streams tokens; 'See inside' renders the real top-k next-token distribution (12 rows, from one forward pass) + the exact constructed chat template. Practical page runs JSON.parse() on the model output against a per-document-type schema (invoice/contract/ticket); wild 'synthetic data factory' parses a JSON array and renders it as a table; multi-model page shows MiniLM cosine scores + Granite's grounded, cited answer over a policy handbook.
rubric.notes: Output-shape handling (streamed tokens; JSON extraction + parse; top-k probabilities) is implemented and rendered; semantic-quality confirmation on Granite specifically rides on the real-inference open item above.

rubric.evidence: conformance-derived + verified in the light screenshot at-a-glance table: declares-model-id (onnx-community/granite-3.0-2b-instruct linked to card + ibm-granite base), declares-task (text-generation), declares-backend ('WebGPU (q4f16) … else the universal WebAssembly/CPU (q8) path'), declares-quantisation (q8/q4f16), inference-off-main-thread (worker.js). Architecture stated honestly: 'Granite — IBM's decoder-only transformer (model_type: granite, GraniteForCausalLM), 2.5B params, 49k-token vocab, 4k context'. License Apache-2.0 (verified via the ibm-granite base repo tags).
rubric.notes: Backend/dtype match what worker.js actually loads (adaptive device/dtype pick). No invented API surface.

rubric.evidence: Driven fresh-profile run: loader in 'download-required' state with a single labelled 'Download model (~2540 MB)' button — no silent transfer of a 2.5 GB model (exactly the 'never silently re-download a large model' rule). Uses the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture. multi-model page shows two independent loaders (MiniLM ~30 MB + Granite ~2540 MB), each honest.
rubric.notes: Absent → labelled Download; the shared loader auto-inits a cached-current copy with no button and offers per-model clear-cache (architecture proven on the qwen2.5-0.5b-wasm sibling). Warm-cache auto-init for this ~2.5 GB model was not separately driven headless — openQuestion.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region (role=status aria-live present), errors-on-the-page (.err status surface + Retry via loader). Generation shows 'Generating on the WASM…' then 'Done.'; failures set status .err with the message. runs-without-webgpu-fallback: requiresWebGPU=false — the universal WASM/CPU path runs with no GPU (the worker upgrades to WebGPU only when a real adapter exists).
rubric.notes: Progress/ready/error wired accessibly on all 5 pages. Offline-cached reuse + a forced partial/evicted cache path were not separately driven this pass (openQuestions).

rubric.evidence: Main page: system-prompt textarea, message textarea, Send/Stop, enterprise example chips (summarise/classify/extract/draft), sampling controls (temp default 0.40 / top_p ranges, max-tokens number, greedy checkbox — greedy disables temp/top_p), Clear, and the See-inside 'Show next-token distribution' button. Basics: input + Ask + chips. Practical: document-type chips + textarea + Extract. Wild: schema-preset chips + spec textarea + row-count number + Generate (renders a table). Multi-model: two loaders + handbook textarea + question chips + Retrieve&answer. Driven verify: every button has an accessible name; no horizontal overflow at 1280x800 or 360x740; primary buttons min-block-size 44px, chips 40px.
rubric.notes: controls-operable is manual-evidenced (driven state check). A full keyboard-only traversal across all 5 pages is the remaining verification.

rubric.evidence: On a fresh profile the loader shows the honest absent-model 'Download (~2540 MB)' state; the transcript/answer/table areas show muted placeholders before a run. No canned/hardcoded result exists anywhere in the source — every output area is populated only by a real on-device run (or an honest parse-failure message).
rubric.notes: No pre-baked output; when a real run has not completed the page stays in its honest download/placeholder state.

rubric.evidence: conformance: images-have-alt (page has no content ), buttons-have-names (driven check: all buttons named across all 5 pages). Status uses role=status aria-live=polite; transcript/answer areas aria-live=polite; textareas have explicit
rubric.notes: Strong baseline. A full screen-reader pass over the streaming transcript, the top-k grid, and the synthetic-data table would further validate.

rubric.evidence: responsive-check: desktop clean · mobile clean. Driven matrix: scrollWidth<=innerWidth at 1280x800 AND 360x740 (DPR3, touch) on all 5 pages (main/basics/practical/wild/multi-model). Mobile screenshot read: crumbs/eyebrow/heading/lede stack, at-a-glance table wraps, no overflow. min-inline-size:0 + overflow-wrap:anywhere on bubbles/flex items; RAG loaders use grid auto-fit minmax(min(100%,18rem),1fr); the wild synthetic-data table sits in an overflow-x:auto wrapper so a wide table scrolls inside its container rather than spilling the page.
rubric.notes: Support recorded ok/ok on the strength of this driven pass.

rubric.evidence: All inference (generation + top-k forward pass) runs in a Web Worker (worker.js) so the CPU/GPU-bound work never blocks the main thread; tokens stream via postMessage. Driven pages stayed responsive with zero console errors and zero failed network requests. inp-stays-low is manual-evidenced (off-thread architecture is the key mitigation).
rubric.notes: A 2B model on the CPU path streams slowly (hence the WebGPU q4f16 recommendation surfaced honestly). An explicit INP measurement while streaming would confirm; the off-thread worker is the mitigation.

guidanceConsulted.query: stream text token by token off main thread without INP long tasks
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Run heavy/CPU-bound work off the main thread and stream incrementally so it doesn't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — all generation and the top-k forward pass run in models/granite/worker.js; tokens are posted to the UI incrementally via TextStreamer callbacks. The main thread only appends text.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; driven run stayed responsive; console clean (0 errors, 0 net failures on all 5 pages)

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel flex wrap no horizontal overflow narrow mobile; wide table scroll
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flex-wrap, gap, intrinsic sizing and min-inline-size:0 on flex items with long unbreakable content; grid auto-fit minmax(min(100%,X),1fr) for responsive rows; wrap wide content (tables) in an overflow-x:auto container so it scrolls inside itself rather than spilling the page.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — composer/controls/chips use flex-wrap+gap; chat bubbles + composer textarea set min-inline-size:0 + overflow-wrap:anywhere; RAG loaders use grid auto-fit minmax(min(100%,18rem),1fr); glance cells overflow-wrap:anywhere; the wild synthetic-data table is wrapped in .tblwrap { overflow-x:auto } so a wide table never causes page-level horizontal overflow. Verified no page overflow at 360x740 and 1280x800 on all 5 pages.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: responsive-check desktop+mobile clean; driven ovf(d/m)=true/true; mobile screenshot read

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status announcements aria-live labelled controls
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce async state changes in an accessible live region; label every control via <label for>; keep buttons named and keyboard-operable; use semantic table markup.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live=polite loader/status regions; transcript/answer aria-live=polite; explicit <label for> on every textarea (visually-hidden where implicit); chips grouped with aria-label; greedy checkbox toggles temp/top_p disabled; the wild table uses <table>/<thead>/<th scope-equivalent> semantics.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance live-status-region=pass, buttons-have-names=pass; DOM inspection in the driven run

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue↔page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied (inherited) — @view-transition navigation:auto in public/styles.css (invariant 6); all 5 pages link the shared stylesheet and crumb back to the catalogue/model page.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: shared design system; crumbs present on every page

openQuestions: Capture Granite's streamed output + tok/s end-to-end on a faster connection or a WebGPU device (the q8 build is a ~2.5 GB download that exceeded the headless sandbox window; the q4f16 WebGPU build is ~1.6 GB).
Keyboard-only traversal across all 5 pages: is every control reachable in a sensible focus order with a visible focus ring, and does Enter-to-send / Shift+Enter-newline behave on a real device?
Drive the warm-cache auto-init (no button) and partial/evicted/offline states explicitly for this ~2.5 GB model.
On a low-memory mobile device, is the 2B q8 WASM path realistic, or should the page steer such devices to a smaller Granite / to the WebGPU path with a memory-pressure hint before download?

followUpGoals.goal: Record a real Granite generation (output sample + tok/s on WASM and WebGPU) and fold it into the real-inference/io-shape evidence, raising both to 5.
Add a Granite tool/function-calling see-inside demo (its enterprise strength): show the model emitting a structured tool call for a described function and validate the JSON arguments.
Add a mobile memory-pressure pre-check + a 'use the WebGPU build' hint before the ~2.5 GB q8 download on constrained devices.

summary: granite is a distinct, honest enterprise demo: IBM's Apache-2.0 Granite 3.0 2B Instruct (its own 'granite' architecture / GraniteForCausalLM, distinct from every other family here) running on-device via Transformers.js — WebGPU q4f16 when a real adapter is present, else the universal WASM/CPU q8 path. Runnability is verified (GraniteForCausalLM registered in transformers.js 3.7.5; the smoke downloaded + began loading with no error; identical worker/pipeline path to the falcon3 sibling that streamed real output). Driven state/matrix pass: honest absent→Download (~2540 MB) state on a fresh profile (no auto-download), real top-k see-inside wiring, clean console/network, no horizontal overflow on desktop or mobile across all five pages (main + basics/practical enterprise-extraction/wild synthetic-data-factory/multi-model policy-RAG), light + dark both render the design system. The one open item is capturing the streamed output end-to-end on a faster connection / GPU device (the 2.5 GB q8 download exceeded the headless window) — no output is faked meanwhile. Support recorded ok/ok. Revision 1.
